blocker_clusters.title: First-User Surface Trust
blocker_clusters.why_it_matters: This cluster controls onboarding/trust honesty on the first-user route and directly affects whether the product can feel release-grade.
blocker_clusters.next_wave_reason: It is a strong candidate for the next bounded wave because it maps to the active I/J prerequisite chain and has open red findings today.
blocker_clusters.not_first_reason: It should not be deferred behind downstream release work because route order still places identity/trust before M/N closure.
blocker_clusters.recommended_wave: Take one bounded first-user surface trust wave over intake, first-runner framing, result clarity, and premium/access-state meaning.

blocker_clusters.title: Content Trust
blocker_clusters.why_it_matters: Content validator truth controls whether authored learning content can be trusted at scale and whether repo state is internally coherent.
blocker_clusters.next_wave_reason: It remains highly controllable because one validator-focused cleanup wave can remove a whole class of failing modules.
blocker_clusters.not_first_reason: It is not always first if first-user trust or route-critical surfaces remain more directly blocking to the current execution chain.
blocker_clusters.recommended_wave: Run one content-trust cleanup wave focused on the currently failing module families and their validator seams.

blocker_clusters.not_first_reason: It should not outrank harder first-user or validator failures unless those clusters are already quiet.
blocker_clusters.recommended_wave: Take one bounded pedagogy/feedback family wave in the currently mixed worlds rather than broad copy churn.
